# ADR-0012 — No "Generated by Claude" attribution anywhere

- **Status**: Accepted
- **Date**: 2026-05-20
- **Decider**: Florentin Dubois
- **Tags**: process, attribution, commits

## Context

Claude Code (and similar agents) by default add an attribution trailer to
their work:

```
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

The user wants this off. Florentin is the GPG signer for every commit in this
repository; the signature is the authorship. Adding a "Generated by Claude"
trailer mis-states the audit trail (the commit was reviewed + signed by a
human; what tool wrote the first draft is not the audit-relevant fact) and
introduces co-author noise into the contributor graph.

## Decision

**No "Generated by Claude" trailers anywhere.** This applies to:

- `git commit` messages (both body and trailer).
- `gh pr create` titles + descriptions.
- `glab mr create` titles + descriptions.
- Issue comments (`gh issue comment`, `glab issue note`).
- Inline file comments referencing the tooling.

The trailers explicitly banned:
- `Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>`
- `🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]…`
- Any variant of the above.

The user's GPG signature on every commit is the sole authorship marker.

## Consequences

- Any commit / PR / MR / issue comment that includes a Claude trailer must be
  amended (or, if it was the head commit, replaced with a new sign-off-and-resign).
- This rule is **global** — it lives in `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and applies to
  every project Florentin works on. The project `CLAUDE.md` reiterates it.

## References

- `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (global rule)
- [`CLAUDE.md`](../../CLAUDE.md) (project-level reiteration)
- [`GUIDELINES.md` §"Commits"](../../GUIDELINES.md)
